evidence_quote: Τυρρηνοὶ γὰρ ἰταλὸν τὸν ταῦρον ἐκάλεσαν
reviewer_note: canonical Etruscan→Greek "bull" etymology; explicit verb ἐκάλεσαν with Τυρρηνοί as subject

evidence_quote: τύρσεις γὰρ καὶ παρὰ Τυρρηνοῖς αἱ ἐντείχιοι καὶ στεγαναὶ οἰκήσεις ὀνομάζονται
reviewer_note: Dionysius glosses Tyrrhenian τύρσις as "walled dwellings" with explicit ὀνομάζονται

evidence_quote: ἀντὶ τοῦ ἀποκρίνασθαι προσαγορεύσαντος αὐτὸν χαῖρε, δεξάμενοι τὸν οἰωνὸν οἱ Τυρρηνοὶ τοῦτον ἁλοῦσαν τὴν πόλιν μετωνόμασαν
reviewer_note: Strabo's Caere/Cisra renaming etymology; "δεξάμενοι τὸν οἰωνὸν οἱ Τυρρηνοὶ ... μετωνόμασαν"

evidence_quote: τὸ τῆς Κύπρας ἱερόν, Τυρρηνῶν ἵδρυμα καὶ κτίσμα· τὴν δʼ Ἥραν ἐκεῖνοι Κύπραν καλοῦσιν
reviewer_note: Strabo: "τὴν δ' Ἥραν ἐκεῖνοι Κύπραν καλοῦσιν" — explicit verb + Tyrrhenian subject

evidence_quote: οἳ καὶ τοὺς πιθήκους φασὶ παρὰ τοῖς Τυρρηνοῖς ἀρίμους καλεῖσθαι
reviewer_note: Strabo: "τοὺς πιθήκους ... παρὰ τοῖς Τυρρηνοῖς ἀρίμους καλεῖσθαι"

evidence_quote: idem populus, si Nortia Tusco favisset, si oppressa foret secura senectus principis, hac ipsa Seianum diceret hora Augustum
reviewer_note: Juvenal: Etruscan goddess Nortia ≈ Roman Fortuna; canonical equivalence

evidence_quote: genus aquilae quam barbatam vocant, Tusci vero ossifragam
reviewer_note: Pliny: "Tusci vero ossifragam" naming the Etruscan term for the bearded vulture

evidence_quote: tellus possessa Pelasgis, quis Aesis regnator erat fluuioque reliquit nomen et a sese populos tum dixit Asilos.
reviewer_note: Silius Italicus: Aesis river → Asilos populus naming — Etruscan toponymic etymology

evidence_quote: quod aesar, id est reliqua pars e Caesaris nomine, Etrusca lingua deus uocaretur
reviewer_note: Suetonius Aug. 97 — THE canonical aesar→deus gloss; "Etrusca lingua deus uocaretur"

evidence_quote: quia ister Tusco verbo ludio vocabatur, nomen histrionibus inditum
reviewer_note: Livy: "ister Tusco verbo ludio vocabatur" — classic histrio/ister etymology

evidence_quote: trecentosque armatos ad custodiam corporis quos Celeres appellauit non in bello solum sed etiam in pace habuit
reviewer_note: Livy: Etruscan-origin name for Romulus's mounted bodyguard; "quos Celeres appellauit"

evidence_quote: ut tres antiquae tribus, Ramnes, Titienses, Luceres, suum quaeque augurem habeant
reviewer_note: Livy: one of the three antiqua tribus (Ramnes, Titienses, Luceres) — Etruscan-origin tribal name

evidence_quote: relicta secunda legione ad Clusium, quod Camars olim appellabant
reviewer_note: Livy: "Clusium, quod Camars olim appellabant" — Etruscan name for Clusium

evidence_quote: relicta secunda legione ad Clusium, quod Camars olim appellabant
reviewer_note: duplicate of idx=1528 from a parallel passage; second attestation, keep both for source diversity

evidence_quote: quia ister Tusco uerbo ludio uocabatur, nomen histrionibus inditum
reviewer_note: duplicate of idx=1375; second attestation of the ister/ludio gloss

evidence_quote: plaga una—Materinam ipsi appellant—non continuit modo ceteros in armis sed confestim ad certamen egit
reviewer_note: Livy: "plaga una — Materinam ipsi appellant" — Etruscan name for a region

evidence_quote: quia ludius apud eos hister appellabatur, scaenico nomen histrionis inditum est
source: Valerius Maximus Facta et Dicta Memorabilia
reviewer_note: Valerius Maximus: "ludius apud eos hister appellabatur" — the histrio etymology in Val. Max. form
